claim_under_attack: Under material ambiguity, the board should approve a decision framework with pre-committed scenario responses rather than a single strategic plan, because the cost of wrong commitment exceeds the carrying cost of structured optionality.
evidence_or_reason: The artifact makes a cost-comparison claim without any supporting financial model, probability weighting, runway data, opportunity-cost estimate, or competitive delay evidence. It even acknowledges that no case-specific evidence was provided and that the 60-90 day optionality window assumes adequate reserves. That makes the framework direction plausible, but the claim that wrong commitment costs more than structured optionality is unsupported.
